mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment growth stays below 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns when either series rebounds within two months, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to positive during tightening cycles, or the mechanism fails to generate negative returns when industrial production drops exceed 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when CPIAUCSL breaches 210.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in periods where inflation remains above this threshold for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises confirms structural labor market weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns when payroll declines are less than 50,000, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly expansions in real gross domestic product directly increase corporate earnings expectations and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 50% during GDP expansion quarters, or the signal rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 0.8 percent signals acute manufacturing contraction and immediate earnings downgrade risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to trigger negative returns when industrial production drops are driven by seasonal adjustments, or positive returns dominate triggered instances by a margin greater than 10 percent.
